Article.ArticleTitle: Strength training and detraining in female dancesport athletes: adaptations and retention of lower limb muscle architecture, strength, and dance performance.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: College of Physical Education, Hubei Normal University, Huangshi, China. jingting.lu@hbnu.edu.cn.
Exercise & Sports Science Programme, School of Health Sciences, Universiti Sains Malaysia, Kubang Kerian, Kelantan, Malaysia. jingting.lu@hbnu.edu.cn.
College of Physical Education, Hubei Normal University, Huangshi, China.
Exercise & Sports Science Programme, School of Health Sciences, Universiti Sains Malaysia, Kubang Kerian, Kelantan, Malaysia.
Exercise & Sports Science Programme, School of Health Sciences, Universiti Sains Malaysia, Kubang Kerian, Kelantan, Malaysia. shazlin@usm.my.
Science of Learning in Education Centre, National Institute of Education, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore, Singapore. shazlin@usm.my.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Ethics approval and consent to participate: All recruitment and experimental procedures were approved by the Human Research Ethics Committee of Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM/JEPeM/KK/23040282) and conducted in accordance with the ethical principles of the Declaration of Helsinki. The study was registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT07164599). This study adheres to the CONSORT reporting guidelines. Written and oral informed consent were obtained from all participants before enrollment. Consent for publication: Not applicable. Competing interests: The authors declare no competing interests.
